title: प्रदोष स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री प्रदोष स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ शिव प्रदोष स्तोत्रम् ॥

जय देव जगन्नाथ जय शङ्कर शाश्वत।
जय सर्वसुराध्यक्ष जय सर्वसुरार्चित॥१॥

जय सर्वगुणातीत जय सर्ववरप्रद।
जय नित्य निराधार जय विश्वम्भराव्यय॥२॥

जय विश्वैकवन्द्य श्रीमन् जय सानन्दविग्रह।
जय शम्भो सदानन्द जय नन्दीश्वरप्रिय॥३॥

जयानन्तगुणाक्रान्त जय भक्तैकवत्सल।
जय सर्वेश सर्वात्मन् जय शर्व कृपानिधे॥४॥

प्रदोषे मम देवेश प्रसन्नो भव शङ्कर।
तव प्रदोषसमये सर्वदा शरणं गतः॥५॥

वृषभध्वज विश्वेश त्र्यम्बक त्रिपुरान्तक।
रक्ष रक्ष महादेव भक्तानामभयङ्कर॥६॥

नमस्ते देवदेवेश नमस्ते भुवनेश्वर।
नमस्ते गिरिजानाथ नमस्ते भक्तवत्सल॥७॥

त्वमेव धर्मः सत्यं च त्वमेव सर्वमेव हि।
त्वामाराध्य गतिं यान्ति ये त्वद्भक्ताः सुरार्चित॥८॥

येन केनापि रूपेण प्रदोषे शिवमर्चयेत्।
स कामान् सकलान् भुक्त्वा अन्ते मोक्षं च विन्दति॥९॥

प्रदोषे शिवमभ्यर्च्य ये नमन्ति कृताञ्जलिम्।
तेषां करस्थमेवात्र शम्भोः सायुज्यमुत्तमम्॥१०॥

॥ इति श्री स्कन्दपुराणोक्तं प्रदोष स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Pradosha Stotram ||

|| Shiva Pradosha Stotram ||

Jaya Deva Jagannatha Jaya Shankara Shashvata.
Jaya Sarva-suradhyaksha Jaya Sarva-surarchita.||1||

Jaya Sarva-gunatita Jaya Sarva-varaprada.
Jaya Nitya Niradhara Jaya Vishvambhara-avyaya.||2||

Jaya Vishvaika-vandya Shriman Jaya Sa-ananda-vigraha.
Jaya Shambho Sadananda Jaya Nandishvara-priya.||3||

Jaya-ananta-gunakranta Jaya Bhaktaika-vatsala.
Jaya Sarvesha Sarvatman Jaya Sharva Kripanidhe.||4||

Pradoshe Mama Devesha Prasanno Bhava Shankara.
Tava Pradosha-samaye Sarvada Sharanam Gatah.||5||

Vrishabha-dhvaja Vishvesha Tryambaka Tripurantaka.
Raksha Raksha Mahadeva Bhaktanam-abhayankara.||6||

Namaste Deva-devesha Namaste Bhuvaneshvara.
Namaste Girijanatha Namaste Bhaktavatsala.||7||

Tvameva Dharmah Satyam Cha Tvameva Sarvam-eva Hi.
Tvam-aradhya Gatim Yanti Ye Tvadbhaktah Surarchita.||8||

Yena Kenapi Roopena Pradoshe Shivam-archayet.
Sa Kaman Sakalan Bhuktva Ante Moksham Cha Vindati.||9||

Pradoshe Shivam-abhyarchya Ye Namanti Kritanjulim.
Tesham Karastham-evatra Shambhoh Sayujyam-uttamam.||10||

|| Iti Shri Skanda-purana-uktam Pradosha Stotram Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
१. जगन्नाथ, शाश्वत शंकर, सर्वदेवाध्यक्ष, सर्वदेवपूजित देव की जय हो।

२. सर्वगुणातीत, सर्ववरप्रद, नित्य, निराधार, अव्यय विश्वम्भर की जय हो।

३. श्रीमान विश्वैकवन्द्य, सानन्दविग्रह, शम्भु सदानन्द, नन्दीश्वरप्रिय की जय हो।

४. अनन्तगुणयुक्त, भक्तवत्सल, सर्वेश, सर्वात्मन, कृपानिधि शर्व की जय हो।

५. हे देवेश शंकर! प्रदोषकाल में मुझ पर प्रसन्न हों। आपके प्रदोष समय में सदा शरणागत हूँ।

६. हे वृषभध्वज, विश्वेश, त्र्यम्बक, त्रिपुरान्तक! रक्षा करो, रक्षा करो, हे भक्तों को अभय देने वाले महादेव!

७. देवदेवेश, भुवनेश्वर, गिरिजानाथ, भक्तवत्सल को नमस्कार।

८. आप ही धर्म हैं, सत्य हैं, सर्वस्व हैं। आपकी आराधना से भक्तजन गति प्राप्त करते हैं।

९. किसी भी रूप में प्रदोषकाल में शिव का पूजन करने वाला सकल कामनाएँ भोगकर अन्त में मोक्ष पाता है।

१०. प्रदोषकाल में शिव की पूजा कर कृताञ्जलि नमस्कार करने वालों के हाथ में ही शम्भु का उत्तम सायुज्य है।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीप्रदोषस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य स्कन्द ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्री सदाशिव देवता। ॐ नमः शिवाय बीजम्। प्रदोष शक्तिः। शिवसायुज्य प्राप्ति कीलकम्। श्रीसदाशिवप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: The primary stotra for Pradosh Vrat — recited during twilight on Trayodashi
Invokes fulfillment of all desires and ultimate moksha
Shiva Sayujya is guaranteed to devoted Pradosha worshippers
Powerful during Maha Pradosha and Soma Pradosha
From Skanda Purana — authenticated by Lord Skanda himself
Traditionally invoked to establish an atmosphere of divine grace during evening worship
description: Pradosha Stotram — प्रदोष स्तोत्रम् — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Lord Shiva. Drawn from Skanda Purana, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Regular recitation is believed to from Skanda Purana — authenticated by Lord Skanda himself, create an atmosphere of divine grace during evening worship, and shiva Sayujya is guaranteed to devoted Pradosha worshippers. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri and Pradosh. The text contains 10 verses and takes around 5 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Pradosha Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Pradosha Stotram from Skanda Purana in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Essential prayer for Pradosh Vrat invoking Lord Shiva's twilight grace.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Pradosha Stotra and when is it recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Pradosha Stotra is a Sanskrit hymn recited during Pradosha Kaal, the auspicious twilight window of roughly one and a half hours straddling sunset on every Trayodashi (the thirteenth lunar day) of the waxing and waning fortnight. Trayodashi falls twice each lunar month, giving the Pradosha Vrat a fortnightly rhythm. The stotra is chanted as the principal devotional offering during the vrat, after the abhisheka and bilva-patra archana of the Shiva linga, just as the lamps are lit at dusk. (Source: Saivism.net Pradosha Mahatmya; Drik Panchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the scriptural source of the Pradosha Vrat and stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Pradosha Vrat is described extensively in the Skanda Purana and the Shiva Purana. The Skanda Purana narrates that on Trayodashi at dusk, Shiva and Parvati are at their most accessible, with Shiva dancing the cosmic Tandava between the horns of Nandi while all devas attend the heavenly court. The Pradosha Mahatmya from the Shiva Purana states that worship of Shiva at Pradosha equals the merit of worshipping Brahma, Vishnu, and all other deities. The Pradosha Stotra crystallises this theology into chantable verses. (Source: Skanda Purana Pradosha Vrata chapter; Shiva Purana Pradosha Mahatmya; Kamakoti.org.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is twilight on Trayodashi called Pradosha Kaal?
seoFaqs.answer: Pradosha literally means 'the dispelling of fault' or 'the onset of dusk' (pra = forth, dosha = fault or evening). In the Skanda Purana account, this window was chosen by Shiva himself as the moment when he forgives all faults of sincere devotees. The exact pradosha muhurta is calculated as the period from approximately 1.5 hours before sunset to 1 hour after sunset on Trayodashi, the bridge between day and night when the deva-loka and bhu-loka are most porous. (Source: Drik Panchang; Amitray Pradosh Vrat resources.)

seoFaqs.question: Are there special varieties of Pradosha?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes, the day on which Trayodashi falls gives the Pradosha its specific character and added merits. Som Pradosham (Monday) is sought for marital harmony and progeny; Bhauma Pradosham (Tuesday) for relief from debt and Mars-related afflictions; Saumya Pradosham (Wednesday) for intelligence and education; Guru Pradosham (Thursday) for spiritual guidance; Shukra Pradosham (Friday) for prosperity; Shani Pradosham (Saturday) is considered especially powerful for relief from Saturn-related troubles; and Bhanu Pradosham (Sunday) for health and vitality. The Pradosha Stotra is recited on all of them. (Source: Pujayagna; Vedic Folks Ashtamurthi Homam article.)

seoFaqs.question: How should the Pradosha Stotra be chanted at home?
seoFaqs.answer: Devotees observe a partial fast (or eat once before sunset), bathe before pradosha kaal, and set up the Shiva puja with bilva leaves, white flowers, ash (vibhuti), and a ghee or sesame oil lamp. Just as the sun sets, a panchamrita abhisheka of the linga is performed, followed by 108 bilva-patra offerings while chanting Om Namah Shivaya. The Pradosha Stotra is then recited, often followed by the Pradosh Vrat Katha, the Mahamrityunjaya mantra (108 times), and Shiva aarti. The fast is broken after moonrise. (Source: My Pooja Box Pradosh Vrat Pooja Vidhi; Onlinepuja.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits do devotees seek from this stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional Skanda Purana sources promise that sincere observance of the Pradosha Stotra and vrat absolves accumulated sins, removes obstacles in marriage and progeny, grants relief from chronic illness and debt, mitigates malefic planetary periods, and bestows moksha. The vrat is often kept for 11 or 26 consecutive Pradoshas as a structured anushthana for a specific intention, then continued lifelong as a regular sadhana. The stotra is regarded as so potent that even one sincere recitation at pradosha kaal is said to outweigh many ordinary days of worship. (Source: Onlinepuja Pradosh Vrat Katha; Saivism.net; Vama.app.)
